title: Kyiv Strikes Russian Oil Refineries Despite Trump's Repeated Requests to Halt
detail: Ukraine carried out drone and missile strikes against Russian oil refineries despite repeated requests from US President Donald Trump to cease such attacks. The strikes signal continued Ukrainian defiance of American pressure as peace talks remain stalled. Trump had urged Kyiv to halt long-range strikes on Russian energy infrastructure as part of broader diplomatic outreach toward Moscow, but Ukrainian officials have argued such strikes are a legitimate tool to degrade Russia's war economy.

title: Zelenskyy Warns Prolonged Middle East War Could Reduce Western Military Aid to Ukraine
detail: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned that a prolonged conflict in the Middle East risks diverting Western political attention and military resources away from Ukraine. Zelenskyy made the remarks as US and European governments juggle competing security demands. His warning reflects Kyiv's ongoing concern that broader regional instability could erode the political will and logistical capacity of Western partners to sustain weapons deliveries and financial support for Ukraine's defense.

title: Slovakia PM Fico Calls on EU to Lift Sanctions on Russian Oil and Gas
detail: Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico publicly called on the European Union to lift sanctions on Russian oil and gas imports, the latest challenge to the bloc's unified energy sanctions posture. Fico, who has previously opposed EU military aid to Ukraine, framed the demand as an economic necessity for Central European states. The call adds pressure to ongoing EU debates over extending and expanding the Russia sanctions regime amid the prolonged war.

title: First Lady Zelenska Facilitates Fourth Ukraine-Russia Child Reunification Exchange
detail: Ukraine's First Lady Olena Zelenska facilitated the fourth child reunification exchange between Ukraine and Russia, continuing the diplomatic-humanitarian track focused on returning Ukrainian children taken to Russia. The exchange represents ongoing engagement on POW and civilian return channels despite the active conflict. Ukraine has accused Russia of the forced deportation and 'Russification' of tens of thousands of Ukrainian children, and the International Criminal Court issued an arrest warrant for President Putin and Russian Children's Rights Commissioner Maria Lvova-Belova in relation to the alleged deportations.

title: Ukraine and Syria Agree to Cooperate on Security and Exchange Military Experience
detail: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that Ukraine and Syria have agreed to cooperate on security matters and exchange military experience. The agreement marks a notable diplomatic outreach by Kyiv toward the post-Assad Syrian government, reflecting Ukraine's effort to build broader international partnerships outside traditional Western allies. The security cooperation framework signals potential intelligence and military-experience sharing between the two countries.

media.caption: Ukraine and Syria Agree to Cooperate on Security and Exchange Military Experience
